metadata.title:
f|[fT5 DEATH


/ IN TIL F|R[

Woman Burned and Two
Men Are Injured at


Burke, Idaho

pages.raw_text:
WALLACE, Idaho, Oct. GA fire
broke out at Burke last night. The
Miners Home lodging house was burn
ed to the ground and Mrs. Brand, who
was asleep IN one of the rooms, was
burned to death. John Snyder and M
R. Freeman, two other dodgers, who
did not hear the cries of "fre" in time,
narrowly escaped with their lives. As
it was, they both were severely burn,
ed. Six Or seven small frame cottages
were also burned before the flames
had spent their fury. For time it was
thought the entire mining camp would
be destroyed, as the fire was almost
impossible to control. ...-


Owing to the peculiar situation OF
the town, the small fire precautions
there were practically useless. All that
saved the town was the fact that the
wind was blowing in such a way that
the sparks. and flames did not take a
direction that would endanger the
whole street.


The greatest excitement prevailed
during the progress of the fire, men
and women crowded each other about
as they, frantically tried to lend aid
to the escaping. inmates of the lodging
house. and carried the furnishings OF
the business houses and residences into
the street as. far from the ravages Of
the damss as possible.


But all their efforts to save the
buildings in the line Of the fire de-
mon were fruitless.


The Miners Home is but small
buihinq of not more than 20 rooms.
